subject: Ecology
title: Synanthropes
description: A quick, practical guide to Synanthropes.

hook.text: Some species don't just tolerate cities—they prefer them. Ecologists call them synanthropes.

buildup.text: Pigeons, cockroaches, and house sparrows have lived alongside humans for thousands of years.

discovery.text: Synanthropes exploit human structures for nesting and human waste for food.

twist.text: Raccoons open garbage cans. Crows solve puzzles. Urban life selects for cleverness.

climax.text: These species are evolving in real time—urban populations differ genetically from rural ones.

punchline.text: Cities don't just attract species. They evolve them.

quiz.question: What are synanthropes?
options: Animals that avoid all human contact
Plants that only grow in forests
